name: Vivox AI
oneliner: Vivox AI builds regulator-ready "atomic" AI agents for AML, KYB/KYC, and financial crime compliance at regulated institutions.
finserv: In live deployments, Vivox has reduced complex compliance case processing times from six hours to ~30 minutes, lowered false-positive screening alerts by up to 86%, and enabled straight-through processing rates up to 50% for onboarding and due-diligence workflows. Backers include Axel Weber (former Bundesbank president and UBS chairman) and Dan Cobley (former Google UK MD), plus senior executives from Barclays — deep regulatory and financial institution credibility at an early stage.
team: Backed by Axel Weber, Dan Cobley, Kos Stiskin, James Berdigans, Startup Wise Guys, and Venture Together. FCA-adjacent investor network signals strong UK regulatory awareness.
thesis: Vivox occupies a narrow but extremely defensible position: compliance agents built specifically to satisfy regulators, not just automate compliance analysts. The "atomic agent" architecture — where each agent handles a single auditable task — mirrors the accountability and explainability requirements that financial regulators are beginning to formalise. This gives you a potential intelligence partner or acquisition target in the compliance agent layer: a UK-anchored team building exactly the kind of regulator-first architecture that enterprise financial institutions need to deploy AI in production. Their AML/KYC focus aligns with the identity and onboarding gap you're monitoring.
evidence: Vivox AI Raises £1.3m to Scale Regulator-Ready Atomic AI Agents for Financial Crime Compliance
